Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are given several tables and we need to create an event log for process mining Steps 1 Identify cases use order_id as the case identifier 2 Derive activities from each table 3 Map timestamps each event should have a timestamp When multiple timestamps exist for one activity like multiple picking events for one order we may create separate events if they are distinct 4 Combine data link events by order_id and ensure chronological order 5 Enrich with resources and attributes

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have the following tables Orders gives the order creation event Order Lines gives picking events for each item Note that an order may have multiple items and each item is picked at a different time by different pickers Delivery gives dispatch and arrival events Note that an order may have multiple deliveries split shipments Invoice gives invoice issued events An order may have multiple invoices Payments gives payment received events for each invoice

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We also have optional tables for Products and Resources which we can use for additional attributes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets define the activities we can create From Orders Order Created timestamp order_date resource sales_person_id From Order Lines For each line item each row in Order Lines we can create an event Item Picked

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Alternatively if we want to model the picking of all items in an order as one event we would need to aggregate But the problem says If multiple timestamps are available eg item_picking_date for multiple items produce one event per activity if it is meaningful or summarize if appropriate Since the picking of each item is a separate activity we will create one event per line item

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: From Delivery We have two events per delivery dispatch and arrival So we can create two events per delivery record Order Dispatched and Order Arrived Note an order may have multiple deliveries so we may have multiple dispatch and arrival events for the same order From Invoice Invoice Issued one event per invoice From Payments Payment Received one event per payment

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets define the columns for the event log Case ID order_id Activity Name one of the defined activities Timestamp the date time of the event Resource the responsible entity for sales picking etc Other Attributes

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Lets use the resource_id and then we can also add the resource name as an attribute if we want But to keep it simple well just use the resource_id

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Order Created Resource sales_person_id Item Picked Resource picker_id Order Dispatched Resource carrier_id Order Arrived Resource carrier_id Invoice Issued Resource billing_person_id Payment Received Resource none

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: For example for Order Created customer_id CUST ABC customer_type Retail order_priority High For Item Picked item_id ITEM 001 quantity 2 item_description Widget A item_category Widgets For Dispatch and Arrival delivery_id DLV 5001 carrier_id CR 01 delivery_note Normal route For Invoice Issued invoice_id INV 1001 invoice_amount 25000 For Payment Received payment_id PAY 2001 payment_method Credit Card payment_reference REF CC 123

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: From Payments create Payment Received events Well join with Invoice to get the order_id Actually the Payments table has invoice_id and we have Invoice table with order_id We can link via invoice_id to get order_id

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets check for missing orders The Orders table has order_ids 1001 1002 1003 1004 1005 Order 1004 does not have any order lines deliveries invoices or payments So we will only have the Order Created event for order 1004

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We must be cautious with the chronological order We will sort the events for each order by timestamp

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now we have all the events We need to sort them by Case ID and then by Timestamp
